title: Transitional Housing/Shelter * Homeless Veterans - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Housing & Shelter > Transitional Housing/Shelter * Homeless Veterans 11 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - Homeless Youth nsitional Housing/Shelter AIDS/HIV Crime Victims Disabilities Issues Homeless Families Homeless Men Homeless Veterans Homeless Women Pregnant Individuals CENTRAL CITY CONCERN HOUSING AND DEVELOPMENT SUPPORTIVE HOUSING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a variety of affordable housing options, including transitional housing and permanent supportive housing. These options combine affordable housing with essential services such as health care, recovery support, employment assistance, and veteran services. Also provides subsidized rental units to help ensure long-term housing stability 523 NW Everett Street Portland, OR 97209 Eligibility: Individuals and families experiencing homelessness, individuals in recovery, those struggling with mental health challenges, living with HIV/AIDS or other disabling conditions, and/or a history of criminal involvement, and veterans experiencing homelessness Hours: Varies by property Email (503) 548-4931 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CENTRAL OREGON VETERANS OUTREACH OUTREACH CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Walk-in center providing referrals, food, clothing, DME, camping basic needs, and water for anyone experiencing homelessness or extremely low-income. Conducts outreach to veterans and non-veterans living in homeless camps by providing potable water, food, clothing, phone/fax services,gas vouchers (once per year), ordering DD214 documents for veterans, and facilitating connection to veterans services. 61510 S Highway 97 Suite 100 Bend, OR 977023706 Eligibility: Veterans who are homeless or at imminent risk of homelessness, who need to be screened for eligibility for programs and connection to services for housing, medical, employment and more. Street Outreach services are available for veteran and non-veteran homeless and low-income individuals and households. Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-3:45pm Email (541) 383-2793 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COLUMBIACARE SERVICES VETERANS RESOURCE CENTER GPD CASE MANAGEMENT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Administers the VA Grant and Per Diem (GPD) transitional housing program, which promotes the development and provision of supportive housing and/or supportive services. The goal of the program is to help homeless veterans achieve residential stability, increase their skill levels and/or income, and gain greater self-determination. 601 N Grape Street Medford, OR 97501 Eligibility: Veterans experiencing homelessness. Veterans do not have to be earning any income to be eligible. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-noon and 1pm-5pm Email (541) 779-8564 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY OUTREACH INC GOOD2GO VETERAN PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Serves veterans and their families by offering transitional housing with case management, peer support, community services, transitional classes geared towards veterans, an advisory group, and collaboration with other VA providers. Average length of stay in shelter is 4-6 months. Also provides homeless prevention case management services for veterans in the community. 865 NW Reiman Avenue Corvallis, OR 97330 Eligibility: Veterans and their families Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 758-3000 Get Directions Visit Website More Details DO GOOD MULTNOMAH THAYER FAMILY FOUNDATION VETERANS SHELTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a transitional housing program with 17 beds. Program participants will have their own private room with a restroom, television, microwave, mini-fridge, and coffee pot. Services include case management, recovery support, hot meals, a food pantry, a clothing closet, laundry services, and transit passes. Consent to substance use testing is required for participation in the program. This program may operate on a waitlist. Eligibility: Veterans who are currently experiencing homelessness and in active recovery from substance use disorders Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week (503) 278-4417 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OPERATION REBUILD HOPE BRYAN'S HOME OPERATION REBUILD HOPE VETERAN TRANSITIONAL HOUSING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides transitional housing and wrap around services by offering clean, sober, and safe living facilities. 1520 Sherman Avenue North Bend, OR 97459 Eligibility: Must be eligible for Veterans Affairs Grant Per Diem (VA-GPD) and pass a criminal background check (no violent criminal history). Hours: Monday-Friday 10am-3pm (541) 808-3266 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OPERATION REBUILD HOPE TIMBERWOLVES DEN OPERATION REBUILD HOPE VETERAN TRANSITIONAL HOUSING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides transitional housing and wrap around services by offering clean, sober, and safe living facilities. 1480 Sherman Avenue North Bend, OR 97459 Eligibility: Must be eligible for Veterans Affairs Grant Per Diem (VA-GPD) and pass a criminal background check (no violent criminal history). Hours: Monday-Friday 10am-3pm Email (541) 808-9025 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SALVATION ARMY VETERANS AND FAMILY CENTER VETERANS AND FAMILY CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a transitional housing center offering a supportive environment for up to 60 veterans and their families for stays of up to 9 months. Services include case management, skill building, job training assistance, sober living quarters, mentoring, meals, and benefits assistance. Individuals must attend a 1.5-hour orientation, pass a UA and breathalyzer test upon entry, be able to care for themselves independently, and agree to remain drug- and alcohol-free during their stay. 14825 SW Farmington Road Beaverton, OR 97007 Eligibility: Veterans who are experiencing or at the risk of being homeless Hours: No standard hours Email (503) 731-3951 Get Directions Visit Website More Details TRANSITION PROJECTS DOREEN'S PLACE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a 90-bed short-term, transitional housing residential program. Provides case management, life and job skills training, and support services. Stays are generally 4-6 months long. Beds at a shelter are not guaranteed. 650 NW Irving Street Portland, OR 97209 Eligibility: Age 18 and older single men and male veterans who are experiencing homelessness. Abstinence from alcohol and drugs is expected of residents; random breathalyzer and urinalysis testing is conducted. Hours: Residential Program: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Resource Center (in person or phone): Monday-Friday 8:30am-7pm, Saturday/Sunday/all holidays 8:30am-3pm; service intakes end 15 minutes before close (503) 280-4700 Get Directions Visit Website More Details VA NORTHWEST HEALTH NETWORK PORTLAND VA MEDICAL CENTER HEALTH CARE SYSTEM COMMUNITY HOMELESS REINTEGRATION SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Community Reintegration Services (CRS) assist veterans who are homeless or at risk of homelessness in developing and maintaining the skills necessary to succeed in community living. Services include: -Vocational Services where veterans can access job search assistance, job coaching, educational guidance, and vocational skill-building resources -Community Shelter and Housing works with community providers to help homeless veterans find local shelters -Outreach to homeless veterans -Case Managed Housing maintains 160+ veterans in case-managed housing 3710 SW US Veterans Hospital Road Portland, OR 97239 Eligibility: Veterans who are homeless or at risk of homelessness Hours: Phone hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Walk-in housing program hours: Tuesday/Thursday 9am-3pm (503) 273-5187 Get Directions Visit Website More Details VA PORTLAND COMMUNITY RESOURCE AND REFERRAL CENTER VA COMMUNITY RESOURCE AND REFERRAL CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides help to veterans in accessing VA and community services. Services may include short-term case management, onsite primary care, access to medical, mental health, and substance abuse treatment, and access to VA housing programs, including Grant and Per Diem program, HUD/VASH, Veterans Recovery House and Supportive Service for Veteran Families (SSVF) if found eligible. These grants do not provide immediate assistance, are sometimes one-time, and may include case management. Help with finding market rate or subsidized housing or linkage to community housing programs. Assistance with expediting VA Benefit claims and access to employment programs such as Supported Employment, Veterans Reintegration program, and State employment. Access to computers, clothing, and hygiene items is available onsite. 308 SW 1st Avenue Suite 155 Portland, OR 972043432 Eligibility: Veterans and their families who are experiencing homelessness or at risk of becoming homeless. Veterans must be present in the household and must be the one to request services. Some services available for Veterans who are not eligible for full VA Healthcare benefits. Hours: -Business hours: Monday/Tuesday/Wednesday/Friday 7:30am-4:30pm, Thursday 10am-4:30pm, closed federal holidays -Walk-in hours: Monday/Tuesday/Wednesday/Friday 8am-3pm, Thursday 10am-3pm. No walk-ins after 2:30pm, better to go earlier in the day. Closed federal holidays. (503) 808-1256 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CENTRAL CITY CONCERN SUPPORTIVE HOUSING CENTRAL OREGON VETERANS OUTREACH OUTREACH CENTER COLUMBIACARE SERVICES GPD CASE MANAGEMENT COMMUNITY OUTREACH INC GOOD2GO VETERAN PROGRAM OPERATION REBUILD HOPE OPERATION REBUILD HOPE VETERAN TRANSITIONAL HOUSING OPERATION REBUILD HOPE OPERATION REBUILD HOPE VETERAN TRANSITIONAL HOUSING SALVATION ARMY VETERANS AND FAMILY CENTER VETERANS AND FAMILY CENTER TRANSITION PROJECTS DOREEN'S PLACE VA NORTHWEST HEALTH NETWORK COMMUNITY HOMELESS REINTEGRATION SERVICES VA PORTLAND COMMUNITY RESOURCE AND REFERRAL CENTER VA COMMUNITY RESOURCE AND REFERRAL CENTER
